radicals-l1-i1.text: Picture a single noodle doing a solo dance on a plate, proudly shouting "Yī am number one!"

radicals-l1-i2.text: A straight line walks into a bar and says, "I'm just here to draw the line!"

radicals-l1-i3.text: Imagine a tiny dot wearing a wizard hat, casting a spell to turn everything around it into polka dots!

radicals-l1-i4.text: Picture a tiny "person" doing a little dance, and every time they twirl, they shout "Rén!"—because they love being the center of attention!

radicals-l1-i5.text: Imagine a chef who can’t stop cutting up vegetables, shouting, “Dah! Oh! I need my knife!”

radicals-l1-i6.text: Imagine a tiny superhero named "Li" flexing his muscles, shouting, "I have the power!" as he lifts a giant dumpling!

radicals-l1-i7.text: Imagine a tired panda saying "Yow! Not again!" as it tripled over bamboo, reminding you that "又" (yòu) means "again"!

radicals-l1-i8.text: Imagine a mouth with a big "K" on it, ready to "Kǒu" out a giant cookie!

radicals-l1-i9.text: Imagine a giant cookie, perfectly enclosed in a big chocolate shell—because that cookie is really "wéi" in a cozy "enclosure"!

radicals-l1-i10.text: Imagine a tiny "turtle" (tǔ) wearing a party hat made of dirt, celebrating its birthday on a giant "earth" cake!

radicals-l2-i1.text: Picture a sleepy "xi" (she) yawning as the sun sets, saying, “Evening is when I finally get to relax!”

radicals-l2-i2.text: Imagine a giant panda doing yoga and stretching its arms wide, saying, "I'm Dà big!"

radicals-l2-i3.text: Imagine a woman juggling two big "N" shaped noodles—she’s definitely a "nǚ-ling" master of multitasking!

radicals-l2-i4.text: Picture a tiny "zi"pping child zooming around with a toy, reminding you that "子" means "child"!

radicals-l2-i5.text: Picture a tiny inchworm measuring inches with a ruler, saying, "Just call me ‘cùn’—the inch champion!"

radicals-l5-i7.text: Picture "short-tailed bird" when you see 隹 (zhuī).

hsk1-l1-i6.text: Picture "to be" when you see 是 (shì).

hsk1-l1-i8.text: Picture "thanks" when you see 谢谢 (xièxie).

hsk1-l1-i9.text: Picture "goodbye" when you see 再见 (zàijiàn).

hsk1-l2-i7.text: Picture "name" when you see 名字 (míngzi).

hsk1-l2-i8.text: Picture "China" when you see 中国 (Zhōngguó).

hsk1-l2-i9.text: Picture "student" when you see 学生 (xuésheng).

hsk1-l2-i10.text: Picture "teacher" when you see 老师 (lǎoshī).

hsk1-l3-i1.text: Picture "to love" when you see 爱 (ài).

hsk1-l3-i2.text: Picture "to eat" when you see 吃 (chī).

hsk1-l3-i3.text: Picture "to drink" when you see 喝 (hē).

hsk1-l3-i4.text: Picture "to look" when you see 看 (kàn).

hsk1-l3-i5.text: Picture "to listen" when you see 听 (tīng).

hsk1-l3-i6.text: Picture "to speak" when you see 说 (shuō).

hsk1-l3-i7.text: Picture "to come" when you see 来 (lái).

hsk1-l3-i8.text: Picture "to go" when you see 去 (qù).

hsk1-l3-i9.text: Picture "to have" when you see 有 (yǒu).

hsk1-l3-i10.text: Picture "to buy" when you see 买 (mǎi).

hsk1-l4-i1.text: Picture "today" when you see 今天 (jīntiān).

hsk1-l4-i2.text: Picture "tomorrow" when you see 明天 (míngtiān).

hsk1-l4-i3.text: Picture "yesterday" when you see 昨天 (zuótiān).

hsk1-l4-i4.text: Picture "now" when you see 现在 (xiànzài).

hsk1-l4-i6.text: Picture "friend" when you see 朋友 (péngyou).

hsk1-l5-i1.text: Picture "是" when you see 是 (shì).

hsk1-l5-i2.text: Picture "的" when you see 的 (de).

hsk1-l5-i3.text: Picture "了" when you see 了 (le).

hsk1-l5-i4.text: Picture "吗" when you see 吗 (ma).

hsk1-l5-i5.text: Picture "不" when you see 不 (bù).
